2026-04-22T14:45:03.4802340Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4802963Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4803425Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4803819Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4804148Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4804399Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4804562Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4804752Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4804944Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4805198Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4805419Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4805727Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T14:45:03.4806006Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

The README was added as a new file in this push.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **No inline code examples in the README** — The Examples section only links to a generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` URL; there are no embedded snippets demonstrating common operations (create, get, list, delete a ConfidentialLedger).\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is a placeholder** — It only contains a link to general Azure SDK docs; it should describe service-specific concepts such as `ConfidentialLedgerResource`, `ConfidentialLedgerCollection`, ledger types (`Public`/`Private`), and SKUs.\\n- **No dedicated snippet-backed sample test file** — There is no `tests/Samples/Sample*.cs` file with `#region Snippet:` tags for the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling to inject into the README.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.ResourceManager.ConfidentialLedger/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the generic \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith service-specific content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n- **`ConfidentialLedgerResource`** — represents a single confidential ledger instance. Use it to get, update, or delete the ledger and to retrieve its URI and properties.\\n- **`ConfidentialLedgerCollection`** — scoped to a `ResourceGroupResource`; use it to create or enumerate confidential ledgers within a resource group.\\n- **Ledger type** (`ConfidentialLedgerType`) — `Public` or `Private`, controls network access to the ledger.\\n- **Ledger SKU** (`ConfidentialLedgerSku`) — `Standard` or `Basic`, determines node count and throughput.\\n- **Security principals** — identities (AAD-based or certificate-based) granted access to the ledger, each with an assigned role (`Administrator`, `Contributor`, or `Reader`).\\n\\nFor general key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET, see [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the placeholder Examples section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith snippet-backed content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Confidential Ledger\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get a Confidential Ledger\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List Confidential Ledgers in a Resource Group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Delete a Confidential Ledger\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.ResourceManager.ConfidentialLedger/tests/Samples/Sample_ConfidentialLedgerCRUD.cs`\\n\\nUse the auto-generated samples in `tests/Generated/Samples/` as reference for the API calls. AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                Properties = new ConfidentialLedgerProperties\\n                {\\n                    LedgerType = new AadBasedSecurityPrincipal\\n                        {\\n                            PrincipalId = Guid.Parse(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;),\\n                            TenantId = Guid.Parse(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;),\\n                            LedgerRoleName = ConfidentialLedgerRoleName.Administrator,\\n                        }\\n                    },\\n                },\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro =\\n                await collection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, ledgerName, data);\\n            ConfidentialLedgerResource ledger = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created ledger: {ledger.Data.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetConfidentialLedgerSample()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:GetConfidentialLedger\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string ledgerName = \\u0026#34;myLedger\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            ResourceIdentifier ledgerId = ConfidentialLedgerResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\\n                subscriptionId, resourceGroupName, ledgerName);\\n            ConfidentialLedgerResource ledger = client.GetConfidentialLedgerResource(ledgerId);\\n            ledger = await ledger.GetAsync();\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Ledger URI: {ledger.Data.Properties.LedgerUri}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListConfidentialLedgersSample()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListConfidentialLedgers\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId =\\n                ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n\\n            await foreach (ConfidentialLedgerResource ledger in resourceGroupResource.GetConfidentialLedgers())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine(ledger.Data.Name);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task DeleteConfidentialLedgerSample()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DeleteConfidentialLedger\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string ledgerName = \\u0026#34;myLedger\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            ResourceIdentifier ledgerId = ConfidentialLedgerResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\\n                subscriptionId, resourceGroupName, ledgerName);\\n            ConfidentialLedgerResource ledger = client.GetConfidentialLedgerResource(ledgerId);\\n            await ledger.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            Console.WriteLine(\\u0026#34;Ledger deleted.\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.ResourceManager.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` has the correct The README was added as a new file in this push.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **No inline code examples in the README** — The Examples section only links to a generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` URL; there are no embedded snippets demonstrating common operations (create, get, list, delete a ConfidentialLedger).\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is a placeholder** — It only contains a link to general Azure SDK docs; it should describe service-specific concepts such as `ConfidentialLedgerResource`, `ConfidentialLedgerCollection`, ledger types (`Public`/`Private`), and SKUs.\\n- **No dedicated snippet-backed sample test file** — There is no `tests/Samples/Sample*.cs` file with `#region Snippet:` tags for the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling to inject into the README.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.ResourceManager.ConfidentialLedger/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the generic \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith service-specific content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n- **`ConfidentialLedgerResource`** — represents a single confidential ledger instance. Use it to get, update, or delete the ledger and to retrieve its URI and properties.\\n- **`ConfidentialLedgerCollection`** — scoped to a `ResourceGroupResource`; use it to create or enumerate confidential ledgers within a resource group.\\n- **Ledger type** (`ConfidentialLedgerType`) — `Public` or `Private`, controls network access to the ledger.\\n- **Ledger SKU** (`ConfidentialLedgerSku`) — `Standard` or `Basic`, determines node count and throughput.\\n- **Security principals** — identities (AAD-based or certificate-based) granted access to the ledger, each with an assigned role (`Administrator`, `Contributor`, or `Reader`).\\n\\nFor general key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET, see [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the placeholder Examples section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith snippet-backed content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Confidential Ledger\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get a Confidential Ledger\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List Confidential Ledgers in a Resource Group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Delete a Confidential Ledger\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.ResourceManager.ConfidentialLedger/tests/Samples/Sample_ConfidentialLedgerCRUD.cs`\\n\\nUse the auto-generated samples in `tests/Generated/Samples/` as reference for the API calls. sure snippets can be compiled\",\"body\":\"The following projects have snippets that do not compile correctly. Owners should make sure their snippets build correctly to make sure that customers copying snippets (and replacing any placeholders necessary) are successful with our sample snippets.\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Build your service directory. I recommend enabling binlogs (`/bl`) and using the [MSBuild Log Viewer](https://msbuildlog.com/) to view the file (default is \\\"msbuild.binlog\\\" in the current working directory, which is ignored by git already):\\r\\n   ```bash\\r\\n   dotnet build eng/service.proj -warnaserror /t:rebuild /p:DebugType=none /p:SDKType=all /p:ServiceDirectory={ServiceDirectory} /p:IncludePerf=false /p:IncludeStress=false /p:PublicSign=false /p:Configuration=Release /p:EnableSourceLink=false /p:BuildSnippets=true /bl\\r\\n   ```\\r\\n2. After you can build cleanly, in your `sdk/{ServiceDirectory}/ci.yml` add the `BuildSnippets=true` parameter:\\r\\n   ```yaml\\r\\n   parameters:\\r\\n     ServiceDirectory: \\\"{ServiceDirectory}\\\"\\r\\n     BuildSnippets: true\\r\\n   ```\\r\\n\\r\\nFor more information and any tips about how I solved some of these problems for Key Vault, see PR #27551.\\r\\n\\r\\n* [ ] samples/CloudClipboard\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/appconfiguration/Azure.Data.AppConfiguration\\r\\n* [x] sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.QuestionAnswering\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/compute/Azure.ResourceManager.Compute\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger\\r\\n* [x] sdk/containerregistry/Azure.Containers.ContainerRegistry\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/core/Azure.Core\\r\\n* [x] sdk/eventhub/Azure.EventHubs\\r\\n* [x] sdk/eventhub/Azure.Messaging.EventHubs.Processor\\r\\n* [x] sdk/eventhub/Azure.ResourceManager.EventHubs\\r\\n* [x] sdk/formrecognizer/Azure.AI.FormRecognizer\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/identity/Azure.Identity\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/keyvault/Azure.ResourceManager.KeyVault\\r\\n* [x] sdk/metricsadvisor/Azure.AI.MetricsAdvisor\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/network/Azure.ResourceManager.Network\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/personalizer/Azure.AI.Personalizer\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager\\r\\n* [x] sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents\\r\\n* [x] sdk/servicebus/Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus\\r\\n* [x] sdk/servicebus/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceBus\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/storage/Azure.ResourceManager.Storage\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/synapse/Azure.Analytics.Synapse\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/template/Azure.Template\\r\\n* [x] sdk/textanalytics/Azure.AI.TextAnalytics\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document\\r\\n* [ ] sdk/webpubsub/Microsoft.Azure.WebPubSub.AspNetCore\\r\\n\\r\\nFor the Resource Manager ones, it seems to be common to try to condition out part of a source file to squash both namespace imports and code. You could slip these into separate sections, but I also see they are in CHANGELOG.md as a sort of migration guide. I recommend considering our existing approach for an `sdk/{ServiceDirectory}/{Package}/MigrationGuide.md` (as cased) if there's enough to warrant more than just a changelog. See [our Secrets migration guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/MigrationGuide.md) for an example.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":
